preview.summary: Co-hosts Mexico face South Korea in Guadalajara in a Group A fixture that could shape qualification. Mexico bring a revitalized attack and home-crowd energy against a Korean side built around technical quality and the experience of Son Heung-min.
preview.homeForm: Mexico carry home advantage as co-hosts, with a sharpened attack led by an in-form striking line.
preview.awayForm: South Korea qualified unbeaten as the standout Asian side but enter with patchy friendly form.
preview.homeKey: Santiago Gimenez, AC Milan's clinical centre-forward leading the line.
preview.awayKey: Son Heung-min, Korea's captain and creative focal point.
storylines: Co-hosts Mexico chase points in front of a Guadalajara crowd
Son Heung-min versus a Mexican defense on home soil
Rematch of a 2018 World Cup group-stage meeting
preview.h2h: Mexico beat South Korea 2-1 in the group stage of the 2018 World Cup, Son scoring a late consolation.
